channel.title: Behavioral Design Patterns on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Behavioral Design Patterns on yexca'Blog

item.title: Visitor Pattern
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash Visitor Pattern: A Behavioral Design Pattern Intent Represents an operation to be performed on elements of an object structure. It lets you define a new operation without changing the classes of the elements on which it operates. Structure Key components: Visitor Declares a Visit operation for each class of ConcreteElement in the object structure. The operation’s name uniquely identifies the class sending the Visit request, …

item.title: Command Pattern
item.description: 📢 This article was translated by gemini-2.5-flash Command Pattern: A Behavioral Design Pattern Intent Encapsulate a request as an object, allowing clients to be parameterized with different requests. It supports request queuing, logging, and undoable operations. Structure Here’s the breakdown: Command: Declares an interface for executing an operation. ConcreteCommand: Binds a Receiver object to an action; invokes the Receiver’s corresponding operation to implement Execute. …
